title: ADR-0001: Polymorphic user-or-org tenancy
verdict_summary: Canonical, ratified ADR (2026-07-07) formalizing decision D-S17 (polymorphic {user|org} tenancy replacing orgs-from-the-start). Every build mandate it carries — the polymorphic tenant principal, no-Clerk-org-for-personal, real-Clerk-org Teams, shared billing+identity principal, and the named UPGRADE-PASS (personal->Teams in-place re-key) — is covered by existing stories. A-TENANCY is in covers-union; the identity tenant-lifecycle pack, the entitlement-record pack, and the CLOUD multi-tenant-enablement pack carry the concrete build. Zero gaps.

components.name: Polymorphic {user|org} tenant + registry
components.quote: A workspace maps to a polymorphic tenant keyed on a {user | org} principal. Tenancy is polymorphic on the principal; no service may assume the tenant is org-keyed. (covered: feature-map L300 'Tenant registry — idp-tagged polymorphic user|org mapping', L639, L678 'User-keyed personal-tenant provisioning ... D-S17'; A-TENANCY in covers-union)

components.name: User-keyed personal tenant (no Clerk org)
components.quote: A personal user gets NO Clerk organization. A personal account is a user-keyed tenant that carries a full wiki workspace and its own entitlements. (covered: feature-map L678 'User-keyed personal-tenant provisioning + polymorphic workflow-ID (FR-W2/FR-W17, D-S17)', L637 CLOUD-clean boot hostname resolver label-0 tenant)

components.name: Shared polymorphic principal for billing + identity
components.quote: billing and identity key on the same polymorphic principal ... one tenant key, not a separate personal key and org key. (covered: feature-map L117 'Append-only entitlement_versions schema + current projection + polymorphic principal', L118 GET /v1/entitlements/{principal_type}/{principal_id}, L65/L364 polymorphic {user|org} principal resolution)

components.name: UPGRADE-PASS / OrgUpgrade workstream (in-place re-key)
components.quote: The personal->Teams upgrade is a named workstream — the UPGRADE-PASS: on upgrade, mint the Clerk org, re-key the tenant in place, carry the existing data and entitlements across the re-key, and start counting seats. (covered: feature-map L302 'Personal->Teams upgrade pass (org mint + tenant re-key) + access-transition isolation', L693 'OrgUpgrade compensating workflow: mint org, re-key tenant in place, carry data+entitlements (FR-W13)', L639)

components.name: Stable workspace_id RLS tenant key across re-key
components.quote: workspace_id remains the RLS tenant key across the re-key — the row-level tenancy boundary does not move, so no RLS-scoped data is migrated. (covered: feature-map L50 'Postgres schema + tenant-RLS + repository-layer tenant GUC', L638 'Transaction-scoped fail-closed RLS ... + cross-tenant CI probes', L699 tenant RLS fail-closed)
